text: nearly users, recommended way get BANC neuron metadata public compiled meta feather ships lee-lab GCS bucket: one file (~55 MB) harmonised per-neuron table ~188 k rows × 75 columns — root IDs, soma side, full BANC annotation hierarchy (flow / super_class / cell_class / cell_sub_class / cell_type), cross-dataset matches (fafb_*, manc_*, hemibrain_*, malecns_*, fanc_*), neurotransmitter predictions, hemilineage, neuropil membership . Schema details dataset documentation. bancr wraps : banc_meta() accessor returns 6 canonical columns used coconatfly (id, class, type, side, subclass, subsubclass). work full 75-column feather directly, point arrow::read_feather() URL . Two alternative sources available advanced users: banc_meta_create_cache(source = "cave") — live read banc_cell_info() + banc_codex_annotations() (needs BANC CAVE access). Use need labels fresher latest GCS snapshot. banc_meta_create_cache(source = "seatable") — pulls draft banc_meta SeaTable. BANC production team . banctable_* functions package sit behind authenticated SeaTable relevant general user; exist support small group people producing BANC annotations. franken_meta() follows convention: default reads public per-dataset feathers (compiled_data/fafb_783/fafb_783_meta.feather, compiled_data/manc_121/manc_121_meta.feather, etc.), column-unions , never touches SeaTable.
code: gs://lee-lab_brain-and-nerve-cord-fly-connectome/compiled_data/banc_888/banc_888_meta.feather library(bancr)  # Download (one-time, cached) and load the compiled meta feather. # No CAVE / SeaTable authentication is needed for this path. banc_meta_create_cache()        # source = "gcs" is the default  # Quick lookups against the in-memory cache all_meta   <- banc_meta() dna02_meta <- banc_meta(ids = "/type:DNa02")

title: R Client Access to the Brain And Nerve Cord (BANC) Dataset
text: ’s get started analyzing descending neurons (DNs) connect brain nerve cord fly connectome:
code: library(bancr) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  # Get all BANC cell type annotations annotations <- banc_codex_annotations()  # Focus on DNa02 descending neurons dna02_neurons <- annotations %>%   filter(cell_type == "DNa02")  # Extract their root IDs dna02_ids <- dna02_neurons$pt_root_id  # Get connectivity data for these neurons. # Default reads the compiled v2 edgelist from public GCS (~285 MB, # one-time cached download). Use version = "v3" for the updated # synapse table, or source = "cave" for a live CAVE query (auth needed). el <- banc_edgelist()  # Subset connectivity by our neurons of interest. The GCS edgelist # uses `pre` / `post` / `count`; the CAVE source uses `pre_pt_root_id` # / `post_pt_root_id` / `n`. dna02_connections <- el %>%   filter(pre %in% dna02_ids)  # Visualize connection strength distribution (column is `count` from GCS, # `n` from CAVE — rename if you swap sources). ggplot(dna02_connections, aes(x = count)) +   geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, alpha = 0.7, color = "black", fill = "steelblue") +   labs(title = "Connection Strength Distribution for DNa02 Neurons",        x = "Number of Synapses",        y = "Frequency") +   theme_minimal()

title: R Client Access to the Brain And Nerve Cord (BANC) Dataset
text: BANC annotation system employs hierarchical classification structure:

title: R Client Access to the Brain And Nerve Cord (BANC) Dataset
text: Anatomical Properties: - region: CNS region (brain, central_brain, optic_lobe, ventral_nerve_cord, neck_connective) - side: Laterality (L=left, R=right) - nerve: Entry/exit nerve (left_antennal_nerve, right_mesothoracic_leg_nerve, etc.) Functional Properties: - cell_function: Brief functional description (antenna_motor, leg_motor, anti_diuresis, etc.) - peripheral_target_type: Target sensor/effector (accessory_tibia_flexor_muscle, chordotonal_organ, etc.) - body_part_sensory/effector: Body regions innervated (abdomen, antenna, wing, etc.) Molecular Properties: - neurotransmitter_verified: Literature-confirmed neurotransmitters unpublished literature review (https://github.com/funkelab/drosophila_neurotransmitters/tree/main) (acetylcholine, dopamine, gaba, dopamine, serotonin, ocotopamine, tyramine, histamine, negative results) - neurotransmitter_predicted: CNN-predicted neurotransmitters - neuropeptide_verified: Literature-confirmed neuropeptides (AstA, CCAP, dNPF, etc.) Cross-Dataset Integration: - fafb_783_match_id: Corresponding FAFB v783 neuron IDs - manc_121_match_id: Corresponding MANC v1.2.1 neuron IDs - hemilineage: Developmental lineage classification (00A, ALad1, LB7, etc.) systematic annotation framework enables: - Consistent terminology across BANC analyses - Hierarchical organisation broad categories specific cell types - Cross-dataset integration Drosophila connectomes (FAFB, MANC) - Multi-modal characterisation combining anatomy, function, molecular properties complete annotation taxonomy detailed term descriptions, see BANC paper supplementary materials (Bates et al. 2025) BANC community wiki.

code: # Show only the portion in the brain an1.mesh.simp <- neuronlist(an1.left.mesh.simp, an1.right.mesh.simp) an1.mesh.simp.brain  <- banc_decapitate(an1.mesh.simp, invert = TRUE)  # Convert to JRC2018F space an1.mesh.simp.brain.jrc2018f <- banc_to_JRC2018F(an1.mesh.simp.brain,                                                   method = "tpsreg",                                                   banc.units = "nm")  # Plot in JRC2018 space nopen3d() plot3d(JRC2018F.surf, col = "lightgrey", alpha = 0.1) plot3d(an1.mesh.simp.brain.jrc2018f, col = c("turquoise", "navy"), alpha = 0.75, add = TRUE) if (!requireNamespace("remotes")) install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github('natverse/nat.jrcbrains')  library(nat.jrcbrains)  ## You may need to download the relevant registration, if you have not already: # download_saalfeldlab_registrations() ## if you previously ran choose_banc() ## now run:  # choose_segmentation("flywire31") # Which directs you towards the active FAFB-FlyWire segmentation  # Define the IDs we wish to fetch # these are from the 783 materialisation (i.e.) published version fw.an1.ids <- c("720575940626768442", "720575940636821616")  # Get neuron meshes fw.an1.meshes <- read_cloudvolume_meshes(fw.an1.ids)  # Convert to JRC2018F fw.an1.meshes.jrc2018f <- xform_brain(fw.an1.meshes, sample = "FAFB14", reference = "JRC2018F")  # Add to plot plot3d(fw.an1.meshes.jrc2018f, col = c("red","orange"), alpha = 1, add = TRUE) if (!requireNamespace("remotes")) install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github('natverse/hemibrainr')  library(hemibrainr) # Read hemibrain neuron hb.an1 <- "706176085"  # Read mesh, divide by 1000 to reach microns hb.an1.mesh <- hemibrain_neuron_meshes(hb.an1)  # Transforms to JRC2018F, divide by 1000 to reach microns for JRCFIB2018F hb.an1.mesh.jrc2018f <- xform_brain(hb.an1.mesh/1000, sample = "JRCFIB2018F", reference = "JRC2018F")  # Add to plot plot3d(hb.an1.mesh.jrc2018f , col = c("chartreuse"), alpha = 1, add = TRUE) # Transform with Elastix an1.mesh.simp.brain.jrc2018f.elastix <- banc_to_JRC2018F(an1.mesh.simp.brain,                                                   method = "elastix",                                                   banc.units = "nm")  # Plot in JRC2018 space nopen3d() plot3d(JRC2018F.surf, col = "lightgrey", alpha = 0.1) plot3d(an1.mesh.simp.brain.jrc2018f, col = "blue", alpha = 0.75, add = TRUE) plot3d(an1.mesh.simp.brain.jrc2018f.elastix, col = "green", alpha = 0.75, add = TRUE)

One must chosen. rawcoords Logical, whether convert raw coordinates nanometers. Default FALSE. chunk_size Integer page size full-table pulls (used rootids = NULL). mitochondria_v1 table millions rows single materialised response trips reticulate's string parser (Error: basic_string); paginating limit/offset keeps response small enough cross R/Python boundary. Default 200000. nucleus_ids Character vector specifying one BANC nucleus ids. nucleus (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cell_nucleus) contains cell body provides stable reference point neuron identification. fallback Logical, default TRUE. primary source errors, automatically retry alternative source emit warning describing happened. title: Read BANC CAVE-tables, good sources of metadata — banc_cave_tables
text: CAVE tables store rich metadata supporting analysis distributed neural control across entire central nervous system. information CAVE infrastructure, see https://www.caveconnecto./CAVEclient/. banc_edgelist returns data frame neuron-neuron connections pre (presynaptic) neuron upstream post (postsynaptic) neuron. edgelist contains synaptic connectivity data crucial understanding distributed neural control behaviour-centric neural modules across brain-VNC boundary. what: Query BANC all-to-all influence scores — banc_influence
title: Query BANC all-to-all influence scores — banc_influence
text: Retrieve pre-computed influence scores BANC neurons partitioned parquet files stored Google Cloud Storage. Influence scores quantify much "seed" (upstream) neuron's activity affects "target" (downstream) neuron's steady-state response, based connectome's synaptic weight matrix (see Bates et al. 2020).

title: Query BANC all-to-all influence scores — banc_influence
code: banc_influence(   upstream_ids = NULL,   downstream_ids = NULL,   const = 24,   min_score = 0,   method = c("arrow", "duckdb"),   local_path = NULL,   force_download = FALSE )

title: Query BANC all-to-all influence scores — banc_influence
text: upstream_ids Character vector upstream (seed) neuron root IDs. NULL, upstream neurons included (use caution — dataset large). downstream_ids Character vector downstream (target) neuron root IDs. NULL, downstream neurons included. const Numeric constant adjusted influence calculation. Adjusted influence = max(0, log(raw_influence) + const). Default 24 corresponds minimum meaningful influence exp(-24) (approx 3.78e-11). min_score Minimum adjusted influence score return. Pairs adjusted influence threshold filtered . Default 0 returns pairs non-zero adjusted influence. method Character, either "arrow" "duckdb". Controls backend used read parquet files. local_path Path local directory containing parquet files. NULL (default), uses cache directory tools::R_user_dir("bancr", "cache"). force_download Logical. TRUE, re-download parquet files GCS even local cache exists. Default FALSE.

title: Query BANC all-to-all influence scores — banc_influence
text: data.frame columns: upstream_id Character. Root ID upstream (seed) neuron. downstream_id Character. Root ID downstream (target) neuron. raw_influence Numeric. Raw steady-state influence score. adjusted_influence Numeric. max(0, log(raw_influence) + const).

title: Query BANC all-to-all influence scores — banc_influence
text: parquet files contain columns upstream_id, downstream_id, raw_influence. Adjusted influence computed --fly log(raw_influence) + const, floored 0. Data read local cache directory. first use (force_download=TRUE), parquet files downloaded GCS using gsutil. Subsequent calls read directly cache. "arrow" method uses open_dataset predicate pushdown scan relevant chunks. "duckdb" method registers DuckDB view parquet files fast SQL-based filtering. small queries (upstream/downstream IDs), methods fast; large scans, "duckdb" may faster.

title: Query BANC all-to-all influence scores — banc_influence
text: Bates, .S., Schlegel, P., Roberts, R.J.V. et al. Complete Connectomic Reconstruction Olfactory Projection Neurons Fly Brain. Curr Biol 30, 3183-3199.e6 (2020).

title: Query BANC all-to-all influence scores — banc_influence

Default "LM data". shader optional Neuroglancer shader string. NULL (default) layer uses Neuroglancer's built-emitGrayscale shader, controlled shaderControls.normalized.range (set via range). range numeric length-2 vector c(low, high) setting shaderControls.normalized.range — source-data intensity window mapped 0..1 brightness. Default c(1, 30), suits LM volumes Elastix-warped + clipped BANC voxel space (B-spline ringing leaves signal bottom tenth uint8 range). Increase upper bound brighter source data; tighten sparse stains. opacity layer opacity [0, 1]; default 0.55 (matching public BANC JRC2018F atlas imported layer). blend layer blend mode ("default", "additive"). Default "additive" LM signal lights overlaps EM rather occluding . volume_rendering one "" (default), "max" "". Required layer visible 3-D Neuroglancer views. Cross-section / orthogonal slice views ignore setting. volume_rendering_depth_samples integer; many depth samples Neuroglancer uses ray-tracing volume 3-D. Default 788 (value public BANC atlas uses). volume_rendering_gain numeric; 3-D volume rendering gain. NULL (default) leaves unset, lets Neuroglancer's UI control interactively. ids optional vector BANC root IDs add "segmentation proofreading" layer ships base scene. url Spelunker base URL starting state extend. Defaults public BANC scene used banc_scene. shorten logical; TRUE (default), POST state via internal banc_shorturl() return shortened URL. FALSE, return long fragment-encoded URL state JSON inlined. open logical; TRUE, open result system browser via browseURL.

TRUE source = "gcs", re-download feather even cached copy exists. use_seatable Deprecated. supplied, TRUE maps source = "seatable" FALSE (old default) maps source = "cave" (previous default GCS). return Logical; TRUE, return cache tibble; otherwise invisible NULL.

title: Create or refresh cache of BANC meta information — banc_meta_create_cache
text: BANC meta queries can slow; caching avoids repeated database access. Rerun whenever labels updated upstream. Three sources supported: "gcs" (default): downloads banc_888_meta.feather public bucket (cached tools::R_user_dir("bancr", "cache")). recommended path almost users; require BANC CAVE SeaTable credentials. "cave": builds cache live banc_cell_info() + banc_codex_annotations(). Requires authenticated BANC CAVE access. Use need fresher annotations latest GCS snapshot. "seatable": pulls -progress draft banc_meta SeaTable. Restricted BANC production team (requires BANCTABLE_TOKEN); rest banctable_* family category.

title: Create or refresh cache of BANC meta information — banc_meta_create_cache
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default: download once, cache locally, then look up banc_meta_create_cache() result <- banc_meta()  # Live from CAVE (needs BANC CAVE auth) banc_meta_create_cache(source = "cave")  # SeaTable (production team only; needs BANCTABLE_TOKEN) banc_meta_create_cache(source = "seatable")  # Use the cache to drive a coconatfly plot library(coconatfly) register_banc_coconat() cf_cosine_plot(cf_ids('/type:LAL0(08|09|10|42)',                       datasets = c("banc", "hemibrain"))) } # }

title: Read NBLAST match results from CAVE — banc_nblast_matches
text: dataset Character, cross-species NBLAST comparison query. One : "malecns" CAVE table: banc_malecns_nblast_v2. Matches male CNS (Takemura et al., 2024) v0.9 dataset, covering complete male central nervous system (~75K neurons). "fafb" CAVE table: banc_fafb_nblast_v2. Matches FAFB (Zheng et al., 2018; Dorkenwald et al., 2024) FlyWire v783 dataset, complete female Drosophila brain connectome. "hemibrain" CAVE table: banc_hemibrain_nblast_v2. Matches hemibrain (Scheffer et al., 2020) v1.2.1 dataset, dense reconstruction half Drosophila brain. "manc" CAVE table: banc_manc_nblast_v2. Matches male adult nerve cord (Takemura et al., 2024) MANC v1.2.1 dataset. "fanc" CAVE table: banc_fanc_nblast_v2. Matches FANC (Azevedo et al., 2024) v1116, female adult nerve cord dataset. ... Additional arguments passed banc_cave_query, including live (default TRUE; set 2 real-time results FALSE latest materialised version).

title: Read NBLAST match results from CAVE — banc_nblast_matches
text: data.frame following CAVE cell_match schema columns: id CAVE annotation ID (integer). pt_root_id Current BANC root ID time query (automatically updated CAVE neurons edited). pt_supervoxel_id Supervoxel ID anchoring annotation segmentation. Stable across root ID changes. pt_position 3D position voxel coordinates (resolution 4 x 4 x 45 nm) identifying BANC neuron. query_root_id BANC root ID time NBLAST run. May differ pt_root_id neuron since edited. match_id Identifier matched neuron target dataset. Format varies: hemibrain/maleCNS use bodyid (integer string), FAFB uses root_783 (FlyWire root ID), MANC uses bodyid, FANC uses cell_id. Mirrored matches prefixed "m" (e.g. "m12345"). score NBLAST similarity score (0-1). Higher similar. Typical thresholds: 0.3-0.4 strong matches. validation Logical. TRUE match manually validated human annotator, FALSE otherwise.

title: Read NBLAST match results from CAVE — banc_nblast_matches
text: tables populated bancpipeline NBLAST workflow (banc-nblast-compile.R banc-nblast-cave.R). compile step runs NBLAST morphological comparisons writes results feather files; CAVE sync step uploads new results removes stale entries (BANC neuron's root ID changed since NBLAST run). CAVE tracks root ID changes via pt_supervoxel_id anchor point, pt_root_id column always reflects current segmentation. Compare pt_root_id query_root_id identify entries may need re-running (neuron edited NBLAST).

title: Read NBLAST match results from CAVE — banc_nblast_matches
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get all maleCNS NBLAST matches matches <- banc_nblast_matches("malecns")  # Get validated matches only validated <- banc_nblast_matches("fafb") %>%   dplyr::filter(validation == TRUE)  # Find matches for specific BANC neurons my_matches <- banc_nblast_matches("hemibrain") %>%   dplyr::filter(pt_root_id %in% my_root_ids)  # Identify stale entries (neuron edited since NBLAST) stale <- banc_nblast_matches("manc") %>%   dplyr::filter(pt_root_id != query_root_id) } # }

title: Summarise the connectivity of BANC neurons — banc_partner_summary
text: Returns synaptically connected partners specified neurons. Understanding synaptic partnerships crucial analyzing neural circuits Brain Nerve Cord (BANC) connectome, revealing distributed control architecture coordinates behaviour across brain ventral nerve cord regions. banc_partners returns details unitary synaptic connection (including xyz location).

title: Summarise the connectivity of BANC neurons — banc_partner_summary
code: banc_partner_summary(   rootids,   partners = c("outputs", "inputs"),   synapse_table = c("synapses_v2", "synapses_v3", "synapses_v1"),   threshold = 0,   remove_autapses = TRUE,   cleft.threshold = 0,   datastack_name = NULL,   ... )  banc_partners(   rootids,   partners = c("input", "output"),   synapse_table = c("synapses_v2", "synapses_v3", "synapses_v1"),   details = FALSE,   ... )

title: Summarise the connectivity of BANC neurons — banc_partner_summary
text: rootids Character vector specifying one BANC rootids. convenience argument passed banc_ids allowing pass data.frames, BANC URLs simple ids. partners Character vector, either "outputs" "inputs" specify direction synaptic connections retrieve. synapse_table Character, name synapse CAVE table wish use. Defaults latest. threshold Integer threshold minimum number synapses (default 0). remove_autapses Logical, whether remove self-connections (default TRUE). cleft.threshold Numeric threshold cleft filtering (default 0). datastack_name optional CAVE datastack_name. unset sensible default chosen. ... Additional arguments passed flywire_partner_summary details Logical. TRUE, attach per-synapse annotations reference tables. synapse_table="synapses_v3" adds mean_score median_score columns (synapses_v3_mean_score synapses_v3_median_score). synapse_table="synapses_v2" adds neurotransmitter_predicted neurotransmitter_probability columns (synapses_v2_nt_prediction_5; note synapses size >= 5 received prediction, smaller synapses NA). Default FALSE. v3, median-score joins substantially slower mean-score joins (order 10x). -op v1.

title: Summarise the connectivity of BANC neurons — banc_partner_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic connectivity analysis sample_id=banc_latestid("720575941478275714") head(banc_partner_summary(sample_id)) head(banc_partner_summary(sample_id, partners='inputs'))  # Research application: Analyze descending neuron control circuits library(dplyr)  # Get DNa02 descending neurons that control walking behavior dna02_annotations <- banc_codex_annotations() %>%   filter(cell_type == "DNa02") dna02_id <- dna02_annotations$pt_root_id[1]  # Find their downstream targets in the VNC dna02_outputs <- banc_partner_summary(dna02_id, partners='outputs') %>%   slice_max(weight, n = 10)  # Visualize the circuit in neuroglancer banc_partner_summary(sample_id, partners='inputs') %>%   slice_max(weight, n = 20) %>%   banc_scene(open=TRUE) } # } if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # plot input and output synapses of a neuron nclear3d() fpi=banc_partners(banc_latestid("720575941478275714"), partners='in') points3d(banc_raw2nm(fpi$post_pt_position), col='cyan') fpo=banc_partners(banc_latestid("720575941478275714"), partners='out') points3d(banc_raw2nm(fpo$pre_pt_position), col='red')  # Compare results between the v2 (default) and v3 synapse tables id <- banc_latestid("720575941478275714") fpi_v2 <- banc_partners(id, partners='input', synapse_table="synapses_v2") fpi_v3 <- banc_partners(id, partners='input', synapse_table="synapses_v3") nrow(fpi_v2); nrow(fpi_v3) # partner overlap length(intersect(fpi_v2$pre_pt_root_id, fpi_v3$pre_pt_root_id))  # Pull v3 synapses with mean and median scores attached (slower) fpi_v3d <- banc_partners(id, partners='input', synapse_table="synapses_v3",                          details=TRUE) head(fpi_v3d[, c("id", "mean_score", "median_score")])  # Pull v2 synapses with neurotransmitter predictions attached fpi_v2nt <- banc_partners(id, partners='input', synapse_table="synapses_v2",                           details=TRUE) table(fpi_v2nt$neurotransmitter_predicted, useNA = "ifany") } # }

what: Transform Points between BANC Connectome and JRC2018F Template Brain — banc_to_JRC2018F
title: Transform Points between BANC Connectome and JRC2018F Template Brain — banc_to_JRC2018F
text: function transforms 3D points BANC (Buhmann et al. Adult Neural Connectome) coordinate system D. melanogaster template brain JRC2018F coordinate system. Transforming JRC2018F helps move data BANC standard reference space comparison light level data. JRC2018F used Janelia Research Campus light-level registered data, including Neuronbridge (https://neuronbridge.janelia.org/). transformation first step enabling users match BANC connectome reconstructions genetic resources wetlab experimentation.

title: Transform Points between BANC Connectome and JRC2018F Template Brain — banc_to_JRC2018F
code: banc_to_JRC2018F(   x,   region = c("brain", "vnc"),   banc.units = c("nm", "um", "raw"),   subset = NULL,   inverse = FALSE,   transform_file = NULL,   method = c("tpsreg", "elastix", "navis_elastix_xform") )

title: Transform Points between BANC Connectome and JRC2018F Template Brain — banc_to_JRC2018F
text: x object containing 3D points (must compatible nat::xyzmatrix). region Whether transform JRC2018F brainspace (default) ot JRCVNC2018F VNC template (alternative). banc.units Character string specifying units BANC space data (input output, depending inverse argument). Must one "nm" (nanometers), "um", "raw" (BANC raw banc.units). Default "nm". subset Optional. logical vector expression subset input object. inverse Logical. TRUE, performs inverse transformation (JRC2018F BANC). Default FALSE. transform_file Optional. Path custom transform file. NULL, uses default files. method Character string specifying transformation method. Must either "elastix" "tpsreg". Default "elastix".

title: Transform Points between BANC Connectome and JRC2018F Template Brain — banc_to_JRC2018F

title: Transform Points between BANC Connectome and JRC2018F Template Brain — banc_to_JRC2018F
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ### BRAIN EXAMPLE #### # Transform points from BANC to JRC2018F transformed_points <- banc_to_JRC2018F(points, banc.units = "nm")  # Use a custom transform file custom_transformed <- banc_to_JRC2018F(points, transform_file = "path/to/custom/transform.txt")  # Where the default transform files are located: banc_to_JRC2018F_file <- system.file(file.path("extdata","brain_240721"), "BANC_to_template.txt", package="bancr") JRC2018F_to_banc_file <- system.file(file.path("extdata","brain_240721"), "template_to_BANC.txt", package="bancr")  ### VNC EXAMPLE #### library(malevnc) library(nat.jrcbrains) nat.jrcbrains::register_saalfeldlab_registrations()  # Get DNa02 axons from the MANC project DNa02s=read_manc_meshes('DNa02') plot3d(JRCVNC2018U)  # Transform into JRCVNC2918F ## nb convert from nm to microns DNa02s.jrcvnc2018f=xform_brain(DNa02s/1e3, reference = "JRCVNC2018F", sample="MANC") plot3d(DNa02s.jrcvnc2018f, co = "red") plot3d(JRCVNC2018F)  # Transform into the BANC DNa02s.banc <- banc_to_JRC2018F(DNa02s.jrcvnc2018f, region="VNC", method="tpsreg") open3d() plot3d(DNa02s.banc, co = "blue") plot3d(banc_vnc_neuropil.surf)  } # }

text: functions use logic wrap code flytable_.* functions fafbseg R package. banctable_set_token obtain store permanent seatable user-level API token. banctable_query performs SQL query banctable database. can omit base argument unless tables name different bases. banctable_base returns base object (equivalent mysql database) allows access one tables, logging service necessary. returned base object give full access Python Base API allowing range row/column manipulations. banctable_update_rows updates existing rows table, returning TRUE success. banctable_append_rows appends new rows table. bigdata=TRUE, rows added directly big data backend using /add-archived-rows/ endpoint. banctable_move_to_bigdata moves rows normal backend big data backend. invert=FALSE (archive), moves rows specified view big data storage. invert=TRUE (unarchive), moves specific rows row_id big data storage back normal backend. Note: big data backend must enabled base functions work. franken_meta() returns BANC project's reformulated views external connectome (FAFB-FlyWire, MANC, Hemibrain maleCNS), re-keyed BANC's annotation scheme (flow / super_class / cell_class / cell_sub_class / cell_type / hemilineage / region / nerve / neuromere / function / body_part / neurochemistry vocabularies banc_meta() uses). row can compared directly corresponding BANC neuron; source-specific identifiers labels retained alongside BANC-shaped columns.

title: Read and write to the seatable for draft BANC annotations — banctable_query
text: Two sources tables supported. default "gcs" reads per-dataset feathers public bucket gs://lee-lab_brain--nerve-cord-fly-connectome/compiled_data/<slug>/<slug>_meta.feather (slugs fafb_783, manc_121, hemibrain_121, malecns_09). authentication required feathers cached locally tools::R_user_dir("bancr", "cache"). recommended path almost users. "seatable" source restricted BANC production team reads -progress per-source SeaTable tables (fafb, manc, hemibrain, malecns) cns_meta base via banctable_query(). requires valid BANCTABLE_TOKEN. "legacy" source reads single, deprecated franken_meta SeaTable backup; longer source truth post-2026-05-15. multiple tables requested, dplyr::bind_rows() takes column-union; FAFB_, MANC_, hemibrain-specific malecns-specific columns survive rows come table owns .

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Do this once banctable_set_token(user="MY_EMAIL_FOR_SEATABLE.com",                     pwd="MY_SEATABLE_PASSWORD",                     url="https://cloud.seatable.io/")  # Query a table: banc.meta <- banctable_query()  # Archive rows to big data backend (requires a view): banctable_move_to_bigdata(   table = "banc_meta",   base = "banc_meta",   view_name = "optic_region_view" )  # Alternative: use view_id instead of view_name: banctable_move_to_bigdata(   table = "banc_meta",   view_id = "0000" )  # Unarchive specific rows from big data backend: banctable_move_to_bigdata(   table = "banc_meta",   invert = TRUE,   row_ids = c("FoDxhChYQSycLm88JZ11RA", "AnotherRowId123") )  # Append rows directly to big data backend: new_data <- data.frame(   root_id = c("720575940626768442", "720575940636821616"),   cell_type = c("DNa02", "DNa02") ) banctable_append_rows(   df = new_data,   table = "banc_meta",   base = "banc_meta",   bigdata = TRUE ) } # } if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default: FAFB + MANC union read from the public GCS feathers. fk <- franken_meta()  # Only the FAFB rows fafb <- franken_meta(tables = "fafb")  # All four source tables, column-unioned all <- franken_meta(tables = c("fafb", "manc", "hemibrain", "malecns"))  # Force a fresh download of the cached feathers fk_fresh <- franken_meta(overwrite = TRUE)  # BANC production team: pull the in-progress SeaTable instead. fk_st <- franken_meta(source = "seatable")  # Legacy single-table SeaTable read (deprecated; still available) legacy <- franken_meta(source = "legacy") } # }

title: bancr 0.3.2 (development)
text: CAVE-table accessors now default public GCS snapshot (neuron_annotations/v888/*.parquet). banc_cell_info(), banc_codex_annotations(), banc_nuclei(), banc_backbone_proofread(), banc_proofreading_notes(), banc_neck_connective_neurons() banc_peripheral_nerves() gains: source = c("gcs", "cave") — default "gcs". Reads v888 parquet snapshot gs://lee-lab_brain--nerve-cord-fly-connectome/neuron_annotations/v888/ (cached locally; BANC authentication required). fallback = TRUE — primary-source failure, automatically retry source warning. Pass fallback = FALSE surface original error instead. banc_cell_info() banc_proofreading_notes() now actually honour rootids argument (previously declared unused); filter applies GCS CAVE branches. first call given source, function emits one-shot message describing data coming switch. Subsequent calls session silent (rlang’s .frequency = "" mechanism). Three new GCS-accessors: banc_metrics() — per-neuron cable / volume / synapse-count metrics compiled_data/banc_888/banc_888_metrics.feather (~7.5 MB). banc_edgelist_split(version = c("v2", "v3", "legacy")) — compartment-resolved edgelist (axon / dendrite / etc.) compiled_data/banc_888/banc_888_edgelist_split_<version>.feather. banc_synapses_enriched(version = c("v2", "v3")) — lazy arrow::open_dataset() handle per-synapse enriched parquet (~9.6 GB v2 / ~15 GB v3). Apply dplyr::filter() dplyr::collect() get data frame. safe_raw2nm_position() now accepts either character form returned CAVE arrow list<integer> form returned arrow::read_parquet() GCS snapshots, GCS CAVE branches share post-processing path. New internal helpers R/gcs_sources.R (banc_source_announce(), banc_with_fallback(), banc_gcs_annotation_parquet()) banc_gcs_compiled_path() factored banc_gcs_compiled_feather() lazy parquet consumers (like banc_synapses_enriched()) can share cached download path. banc_edgelist() now defaults source = "gcs", reading pre-computed compiled_data/banc_888/banc_888_edgelist_simple_<version>.feather public bucket (auth needed; ~285 MB v2, ~336 MB v3, cached locally). New version = c("v2", "v3") argument selects paper-version synapses (v2, default) vs updated synapses_v3 edgelist. source = "cave" preserves previous CAVE materialised view query callers need live data; auto-derived view name synapses_<version>_backbone_proofread_and_peripheral_nerves_counts, overridable via edgelist_view. Returned schema follows source: pre, post, count, norm, post_count, pre_count GCS; pre_pt_root_id, post_pt_root_id, n CAVE. banc_meta_create_cache() now defaults source = "gcs", reading public compiled meta feather gs://lee-lab_brain--nerve-cord-fly-connectome/compiled_data/banc_888/banc_888_meta.feather. needs BANC authentication avoids slow CAVE union banc_cell_info() + banc_codex_annotations(). source = "cave" keeps previous CAVE-derived behaviour callers need labels fresher GCS snapshot. source = "seatable" reads draft banc_meta SeaTable restricted BANC production team. dead SQL bug branch (renaming super_class despite never selecting ) fixed. use_seatable argument kept deprecated alias. franken_meta() likewise defaults source = "gcs", reading per-dataset compiled feathers compiled_data/<slug>/<slug>_meta.feather (fafb_783, manc_121, hemibrain_121, malecns_09). source = "seatable" (formerly "split" default) source = "legacy" remain production-team callers. New internal helper banc_gcs_meta_feather() (downloads + caches compiled meta feather tools::R_user_dir("bancr", "cache")). Adds soft dependency arrow package, already Suggests. README: new “Getting BANC meta data” section advertising GCS feather main route almost users, clarifying banctable_* family BANC production team .

title: bancr 0.3.1
text: Repository moved flyconnectome/bancr natverse/bancr. old URL continues redirect, please update local clones git remote set-url origin git@github.com:natverse/bancr.git Remotes: lines downstream packages. banc_all_synapses() retargeted gs://lee-lab_brain--nerve-cord-fly-connectome/neuron_connectivity/v888/ new version argument (defaults "v2"; "v1" deprecated, "v3" testing). dead rawcoords min_size arguments dropped help page now documents version’s CAVE coordinate space. New inst/CITATION BANC paper (Bates et al. 2025, bioRxiv 2025.07.31.667571) natverse paper. License field updated GPL (>= 3) via usethis::use_gpl3_license().

title: bancr 0.3.0
text: new banc_lm_scene() overlaying precomputed light-microscopy image layers top public BANC Neuroglancer scene, optional shortened-URL POST nglstate/api/v1/post. new banc_influence() family (banc_influence_arrow, banc_influence_duckdb, banc_influence_path) connectome influence scoring parquet snapshots GCS. read BANC synapses_v3; details = TRUE, banc_synapses() now returns neurotransmitter prediction scores. bancsee() gains clean_segments option surfaces NBLAST scores alongside synapse data; assorted scene-building fixes. read NBLAST match CAVE tables via banc_nblast_matches(). fix Linux PATH_MAX crash Neuroglancer URL encoder scene JSON long. internal: rework CI use system Python via actions/setup-python (reticulate miniconda env longer ships pip), clean R CMD check Rd cross-reference / \usage warnings. Full Changelog: https://github.com/flyconnectome/bancr/compare/v0.2.1…v0.3.0
